content: Same issue on Readm 8  with image_picker 0.6.7+17

Same issue in image_picker: ^0.6.7+17 (Asus Zen phone max pro) 
My app uses landscape mode. Maybe this is the reason ?

@xster We have already made several attempts to correct this problem, the Flutter team has also made several attempts, both to no avail.

After a long time without answers and to alert the Flutter team about the seriousness of the problem that the community has been facing, we try to mark the names of some people who are always going through git and certainly helping us, but unfortunately also without answers.

Seeing that the bug has been open and unanswered for a long time, could you tell us if this bug will be corrected by the flutter team and, if so, the prediction of when it might happen?

We will be happy if you can answer us, thanks!

Hey  guys: 
I solve it  but  I don't know that principle

just  add this before your action


Example:
```
IconButton(icon: Icon(Icons.camera_alt_outlined), onPressed:(){

                      FocusScope.of(context).requestFocus(FocusNode());    //Here is point
                      getImageFromCamera();
                      
})
```

Hope can help u
Tried it but it doesn't change anything.
Same Camera problem with realme c11
It doesn't occur in virtual device, but always lost connection with real device.
Same issue on iOS simulator. The device loses connection the SECOND time I prompt the image picker.

> Same issue on iOS simulator. The device loses connection the SECOND time I prompt the image picker.

Same here

Also tried `FocusScope.of(context).requestFocus(FocusNode());` but it didn't work.
But what I've noticed is if I manually tap any TextField between the image picker calls the app works with no errors. But the moment  I call the picker without manually changing the focus to any TextField, the error appears again.

I also have this issue on Redmi 9s
 But in Samsung s9 it works fine

This problem can be appeared in any time. 
The reason for this is Android OS can kill restart your app in its demand.

Probably, the most best way to tackle this problem can be to save selected images by user to directory and get images from this temporary if app is restarted (killed by OS and restart by it).

I understand, but that would be just accepting to live with this problem.

I believe that the best way to solve this is with the correction of the plugin, it is causing the application to break.

If your application terminates unexpectedly, what is the user's feeling for the product?

Recovering a temporary image after the application is unexpectedly terminated, is just saying that, we accept this behavior.

Amazing that we still have this problem since 2019. 

Has anyone found an alternative to image_picker?

> É incrível que ainda tenhamos esse problema desde 2019.
> 
> Alguém encontrou uma alternativa para image_picker?

**[file_picker](https://pub.dev/packages/file_picker)**

> > É incrível que ainda tenhamos esse problema desde 2019.
> > Alguém encontrou uma alternativa para image_picker?
> 
> **[file_picker](https://pub.dev/packages/file_picker)**

Thank you! Gonna check it out

Verified this issue using latest stable version and [latest](https://pub.dev/packages/image_picker) plugin version. I was able to replicate the `lost connection with device` while testing on Samsung Android 8.1.0 device, but not with Samsung S10 device. Also, the issue isn't replicable straight-away, occurred 3 out of 4 times I tried.
Steps I carried out were:

1. Tap camera icon.
2. Take picture (preferably rear camera).
3. Tap on Retry and repeat step 2.
4. Repeat step 3 few times ( approximately 5 or 6 times, may vary).
5. Tap on OK button after repeating step 4.
6. App shows black screen and `lost connection with device` in console.

same bug

Hey, I kinda fixed the issue in some ways. After much profiling, searching through comments and issues, and not finding any solution y gave up. 

But then I thought it might have something to do with flutter caching images that did not need to be cached. Making use of image cache I could prevent caching of images, and the issue disappeared, at least in my application using Samsung A10.

Hope this helps in some way.

> Hey, I kinda fixed the issue in some ways. After much profiling, searching through comments and issues, and not finding any solution y gave up.
> 
> But then I thought it might have something to do with flutter caching images that did not need to be cached. Making use of image cache I could prevent caching of images, and the issue disappeared, at least in my application using Samsung A10.
> 
> Hope this helps in some way.

Hello. Please explane. What i need to do?

Look into Flutter [ImageCache](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/painting/ImageCache/ImageCache.html) class, it is exposed globally via `imageCache`

In my case I just told image cache that I did not want to cache any image (just to test if it fixed the issue), you should cache only stuff you need to cache.

i am trying to upgrade the version on image picker but as my app is an e-commerce app and the imagepicker dependencies are going further..it is suggesting me to update on plugin then other and it goes on...and when i do these ..my app does work it crashes...so i turned again to my previous version....pls suggest some alternatives....

This is really a serious issue. Do anyone know what to do with this?

```flutter clean``` it just worked fine.
Even with multiple attempts to select the image,I have tested it on these  devices  Redmi k20 pro, mi4i, Redmi6a, iPhone 6,iPhone 11, emulator and simulator and it worked pretty well both in debug and release mode.

As of today, I am using ```image picker 0.6.7+7``` without any issues hope this helps someone

Evident that the problem exists, but, I believe there is a flow that makes this problem happen, if you can, post your code here, we can see the difference between yours and our code, it is possible?

If possible post a **main.dart** with the complete code, preferably using some form fields.

I am 99% sure that the problem is due to incompatibilities with other packages.  My project was running fine, in several devices, and after adding more stuff, image picker crashes EVERY TIME I try to take a photo or pick a image. I'll continue debugging and will let you know once I figure it out

EDIT: The problem was something else I had messed up (extra space in the info.pls string asking for permission). 
The library (0.6.7 + 22) is working perfectly so far in all devices tested (not an extensive list, but quite a few different models like galaxies and iphones)
Even I believe the issue isn't directly with the imagePicker

by the way here's a [sample code to try](https://gist.github.com/maheshmnj/666de97c013a804b8b343966fca0941c) if anyone thinks there's an issue with the image picker plugin itself. This example was run using ```image_picker: ^0.6.7+7```

Update: 
I upgraded the repository to Flutter 2.0 and upgraded to ` image_picker: ^0.7.4`. And still I am facing the issue with Redmi devices. 
What exactly happens is, App opens the Camera Intent and destroys the current Activity of Main App. Clicking picture takes the use back to the MainActivity which was destroyed and App starts afresh.
Yes, many of Redmi divices do not working with this plugin.

To those suggesting that it is because of a conflict with other packages I
dont think that's right.

I created an app with Image picker alone to test it out but it does the
same. It crashes with no defined error. I tried on Huawei, Tecno and
Samsung.

I suspect it conflicts with either:
- Device type
- Memory size
- Android API

Those running it successfully kindly share the 3 details above of the
device you are testing on.

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/48016#issuecomment-817844024 I reproduced the issue here. The privacy descriptions were missing! For IOS, it seems like you should add privacy descriptions into **Info.plist** for both camera usage and photo library access. The descriptions must be defined according to your usage reason. @yadames I just did what is instructed in their pub.dev : 

> iOS #
Add the following keys to your Info.plist file, located in <project root>/ios/Runner/Info.plist:

`<key>NSCameraUsageDescription</key>
    <string>This app needs camera access for generating vocabulary</string>
    <key>NSPhotoLibraryUsageDescription</key>
    <string>This app  needs access to save your photos in the gallery</string>`
    

> Android 
> API < 29
> No configuration required - the plugin should work out of the box.
> 

> API 29+
> Add android:requestLegacyExternalStorage="true" as an attribute to the <application> tag in AndroidManifest.xml. The attribute is false by default on apps targeting Android Q.
>
